Description: External server used to deliver TrickBot DLL payload via HTTP GET request
Suspected TrickBot command-and-control server — received POST beacon with victim host data
Internal infected workstation — source of malicious download and C2 communication
Hostname of the infected Windows workstation identified via DHCP and SMB traffic
MAC address of infected host NIC mapped via ARP filter to IP 10.8.19.101
Windows user account active on infected host — identified via NTLMSSP authentication packets
Malicious payload delivered via HTTP GET — disguised as PDF but confirmed as Windows PE executable
True filename of malicious payload — Windows Dynamic Link Library (TrickBot Trojan)
MD5 hash of the TrickBot DLL payload (ooiwy.pdf / ooiwdy.dll)
SHA-1 hash of the TrickBot DLL payload (ooiwy.pdf / ooiwdy.dll)
SHA-256 hash of the TrickBot DLL payload (ooiwy.pdf / ooiwdy.dll)
MZ magic bytes confirming ooiwy.pdf is a Windows Portable Executable despite PDF extension
Full URL used to deliver the TrickBot payload to the infected host via HTTP GET
Non-browser User-Agent string used during payload download — indicates scripted or automated execution
Trojan malware family confirmed via VirusTotal multi-engine scan — known for credential theft and ransomware staging
